Amarante '26 Week 01

Week 01 20 - 24 May

01

Landing Week

Scroll
Welcome

Arriving in Amarante

First day in Portugal. Suitcases, nerves, and the excitement of seeing the cobblestone streets of Amarante for the first time. This is where it all begins.

The journey started at 3 in the morning, leaving Blanes to catch the plane in Barcelona. The flight to Porto departed at 7:00, so we took our time — once at the airport, we only needed to drop off our luggage and board.

When we arrived in Porto, Tânia came to pick us up and we made the trip to CJ (Casa de Juventude) to see where we'd be staying for the next 2 months in Amarante.

On that first day we already met many people from different countries who were volunteering or doing internships in Amarante. Also trying for the first time the CJ meals, we will get used to it, but the idea of eating vegetarian food 6 times a week was not very exciting.

This first landing week was mostly about settling in, getting to know the place, and meeting the other participants. One of the participants called Ali made us a tour of the city and we visited some of the main buildings, like the church of São Gonçalo and the Amarante bridge.

We already had some fun, like playing football, people that study animation doing own activities... This first 4 days were really entertaining!

And the first weekend more specifically Sunday we went to visit the city of Porto! It looks like we'll be doing loads of activities with the other participants. We're beginning to discover Portuguese culture and sample the typical food, like the famous francesinha.

To see more photos of this week, check out the gallery!

Photos
